chunk_summary: Sustained recovery criteria for MAM include anthropometric stability, absence of oedema, and a clear discharge plan with community monitoring to prevent relapse.
learning_objectives: Understand the criteria for sustained recovery in moderate acute malnutrition (MAM).
Learn the importance of dual anthropometric metrics (MUAC and WHZ/WLZ) in discharge planning.
Recognize the role of community-based monitoring in relapse prevention after MAM recovery.
Identify operational workflows for MAM management in Indian healthcare settings.
key_facts: WHO 2023 guidelines discourage using percentage or absolute weight gain as exit criteria for MAM recovery.
Recovery is defined as MUAC ≥125 mm and WHZ/WLZ ≥ -2 maintained for at least two consecutive visits.
No bilateral pitting oedema must be present on at least two consecutive visits before discharge.
Indian workflows often prioritize WHZ plus oedema for programme entry but recommend recording both MUAC and WHZ/WLZ at discharge.
Discharge planning must include a feasible feeding plan, caregiver education, and scheduled community monitoring.
reference_quote: The 2023 WHO wasting guideline discourages using percentage weight gain or absolute weight gain as outpatient exit criteria and instead requires sustained anthropometric recovery.
